meta.purpose: Seed profile for generating personalized, age-appropriate games for Josh (age 4). Companion to JOSH_PROFILE.md.
meta.privacy: PUBLIC REPO — no PII beyond first names. No last name, address, phone, birth year, or school/teacher name. Any real personal details (address/phone for a safety-practice game) must be injected privately at runtime and never committed.
meta.sourceOfTruth: Montessori progress report, June 2026 (Primary / Level 2, age 4), plus a private worksheet-factory design spec.
meta.resyncWhen: A newer school assessment arrives — re-bucket the `skills` section.

levelKey.W: Working — the CHALLENGE sweet-spot; spend most games here

child.name: Josh
child.birthdayTheme: Feb 14 (Valentine's Day) — low-risk hook for birthday/themed games; no birth year on purpose
child.heritage: half White / half Chinese — for inclusive character design only
teacherNotes: Very observant and curious; loves learning new topics.
Has made good friendships; enjoys working on puzzles WITH friends.
Comfortable in known environments; needs more adult guidance / gentle onboarding with unfamiliar things.
Confident and happy; interacts positively with peers and adults.

friendUsage: Rotate a DIFFERENT friend into each game/level's story, characters, and co-op 'other player'. Vary the content (scenes, counts, word lists) too, not just the name.

characterRoster.numberblocks: Number characters 1-10+ that literally grow/build as he counts or adds — perfect skin for math.

interests.tone: bright, friendly, celebratory, silly-not-scary, lots of 'you did it!' energy

personalization.keepPrivateInjectAtRuntime: Last name, home address, ZIP, phone numbers, school/teacher names. Learning to recite one's address & phone is a real safety skill, so a 'say my address/phone' practice game is welcome — but the parent supplies those values privately; never hard-code or commit them here.

designLaws.nonReaderFirst: He can barely read. Instructions come from AUDIO narration + pictures + a demonstrated example. Games can use VOICE — make it the primary instruction channel. Any child-facing word must be short & phonetic (cat/dog/sun).
designLaws.iconInstructionStrip: Lead each task with 2-3 big action icons (e.g. look -> do -> smile); pictures carry the meaning.
designLaws.showDontTell: Pre-complete/animate the first item as a worked example so he copies the pattern.
designLaws.shapeIsTheInstruction: Dashed line = trace it; ghosted gray = complete/color it; green start -> red stop = path direction. Reuse the same visual language every time.
designLaws.picturesNotWordProblems: Show countable objects (red + blue = ?), never 'River had 1 block...'.
designLaws.oneTaskPerScreen: Never mix two activities that each need explaining.
designLaws.pictureMustNameItself: On naming tasks (first sound/rhyme), the picture's natural 4yo name must BE the answer. Avoid ambiguous icons (chicken!=hen, soccer-ball!=ball, thumbs-up!=thumb).

engagementPrinciples: Transformation over decoration: build the reward INTO the task (the work completes a picture / suits up a hero / moves a friend home). Reward art must NOT carry the answer.
2-player / co-op whenever natural: take turns, race-or-build together, turn indicator so no adult referees. HIS TOP LEVER.
Personalize cheaply: Feb-14 birthday, friends, heroes.
Autonomy & identity: pick-your-hero, build-your-own, 'I did it' certificate, calm-down beat — pair with hard content.
Respect the ceiling: short 5-10 min sessions, big targets, forgiving input, no punishing time pressure.

difficultyTargets.sprinkle: occasionally introduce one gently-scaffolded [P] new skill
difficultyTargets.rationale: Most games sit on his [W] growth edges (the sweet spot); a [M] confidence/reward slice protects motivation; new [P] skills come one-at-a-time with a walkthrough.

notAssessed: money / coin values — BRAND NEW; start from 'penny = 1 cent, count the pennies', then nickels, then compare amounts

mastered: fill-in words / read familiar books from memory
literacy.note: Keep connected text audio-supported. A single short phonetic word + picture is fair game.

geographyScienceSensorial.opportunity: Science is all [M] and UNDER-USED — a fresh, confidence-building domain (sink/float, magnet, living/non-living sorters).

working: write his name and personal details (address/phone — inject values privately, never commit)
practicalMotorSEL.implication: Tracing/coloring/cutting are mastered -> use as calm/confidence fillers, not the main challenge. Frustration & confidence are working -> no-lose design + celebration + calm-down beats.

logicReasoning.status: Recently introduced via worksheets and landed well; screen-native winners. Treat [P]->[W]: scaffold once, then favorites.

gameMechanicMenu.idea: count-and-feed / count-and-tap N objects
Numberblock builder — stack exactly N; character grows
finger-trace the numeral, it animates to life
skip-count hop along stones; fill missing numbers
golden-bead build: drag ten-bars + ones
combine two bead groups, count the total
take-away animation; count what's left
set-the-clock hand / order the daily routine
coin shop — count pennies to buy a sticker; scaffold from zero
fill 10 + some more; name the teen
more/fewer, taller/shorter, heavier/lighter tap
first-sound sort (audio names picture)
build-a-word: tap 3 letter-sounds under a picture
connect two rhyming pictures
hear a word, tap the matching one of 3
sort pictures by sh/ch/th sound
listen-and-answer / picture-story sequence
drag continent to globe; 'where do WE live?' star
color/sort land vs water, island/lake
sorter: living/non-living, sink/float, magnetic, plant/animal
days/months/seasons order & match
match 3D solid to its real-world object
jigsaw, any theme
odd-one-out / sudoku / sequence / spot-diff / shadow / pattern / dot-to-dot / color-by-number
trace-the-path maze: green start -> red stop
breathing star / calm corner — no wrong answer
'I did it!' certificate — sign name, color badge, celebrate
2-player take-turns race/build; turn indicator; no losing

inputUX.instruction: voice-first; auto-play spoken prompt + persistent replay button; never gate on reading
inputUX.gestures: tap and single-finger drag/trace only; NO multi-touch, pinch, precise timing, or reflex challenges
inputUX.feedback: immediate + celebratory on correct (happy sound + visible transformation); gentle 'try again' on wrong (soft bump, keep object in play, no score loss)
inputUX.failure: NO hard-fail / game-over / shame; friendly races still end in 'great try, play again!'
inputUX.session: short ~5-10 min; stop anytime with progress saved
inputUX.onboarding: guided worked walkthrough the FIRST time a mechanic is new (he needs easing into unfamiliar formats); familiar formats jump straight in

avoid: ambiguous pictures on naming tasks (verify the picture names itself)
hard-fail / 'you lose' / shame feedback (harms frustration tolerance)
precise or fast input (pinch, tiny drag targets, reaction-time)
word problems / story math to be read (show countable pictures)
repetition across sessions (rotate friends, themes, scenes, number/word sets)
